name: Sonny Terry
abstract: Saunders Terrell (October 24, 1911 – March 11, 1986), known as Sonny Terry, was an American Piedmont blues and folk musician, who was known for his energetic blues harmonica style, which frequently included vocal whoops and hollers and occasionally imitations of trains and fox hunts.

about_quotes.speaker: William Russell
about_quotes.evidence: The young John Hammond likes Sonny Terry. He did a duet with him... commercially, certainly, would outsell any of the Sonny Terry, I guess, stuff now.

about_quotes.speaker: Percy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: The Potting Shed, which Sonny Terry and Brownie McGee, Leadbelly and all those people played there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Percy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Schaap
about_quotes.evidence: Sonny Terry, who was my neighbor. I used to hit home runs, whiffle ball into his yard.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Schaap interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Ernest "Punch" Miller
about_quotes.evidence: No, I don't know his name, but I can't call it now. Sonny Boy Williams? Mm-mm.
